architecture_summary: A slot-based controller that keeps key, query, and content roles separate while exposing one risky residual bypass.
assumptions: The example uses an explicit map φ from query-space to key-space.
The residual branch is present both during training and inference.

rows.notes: Content is directly supervised; keys are only affected through the updater.
The bypass branch reaches the decoder directly.

shortcut_paths.risk_summary: The residual path can weaken the intended memory bottleneck.

invariants.statement: Persistent key state is not mixed with live query state without an explicit cast.
invariants.boundary: The update is structurally safe only if φ is explicit and documented.

invariants.statement: Direction normalization is only defined when ||x|| > 0.
invariants.boundary: The example mentions normalization but does not prove the nonzero condition.

train_infer_congruence.train_path: teacher-forced context updates key_update during training
train_infer_congruence.infer_path: self-generated context updates key_update during inference
mismatch_points: teacher forcing is unavailable at inference
train_infer_congruence.notes: The updater is not fully congruent across train and inference.

findings.summary: The key updater is only structurally valid if query-space is explicitly transported into key-space.
findings.boundary: This is a structural risk, not a proof of benchmark failure.
findings.recommended_action: Make φ explicit in the architecture spec and code boundary.

findings.summary: The residual bypass gives the decoder a direct route around the intended memory bottleneck.
findings.boundary: Path dominance still needs empirical measurement.
findings.recommended_action: Gate, ablate, or regularize the bypass branch.

findings.summary: The key updater sees different context regimes in training and inference.
findings.boundary: The report does not quantify the mismatch effect.
findings.recommended_action: Reduce teacher forcing or simulate infer-time recursion during training.

formalization_candidates.natural_language_claim: If the key normalizer is an orthogonal projection onto a subspace, applying it twice equals applying it once.
formalization_candidates.reason_it_is_formalizable: This is a local linear-algebraic/idempotence property.
side_conditions: the projection target is a well-defined subspace

formalization_candidates.natural_language_claim: The norm of a residual sum is bounded by the sum of the norms.
formalization_candidates.reason_it_is_formalizable: This is a standard normed-space inequality.
side_conditions: all operands live in the same normed space

empirical_only_claims.claim: The architecture improves retrieval quality on real workloads.
empirical_only_claims.why_empirical: This depends on data, training, and benchmark protocol.

empirical_only_claims.claim: The residual bypass improves serving latency.
empirical_only_claims.why_empirical: Latency depends on implementation, hardware, and batch regime.
